Wells disappeared July 4 after traveling by boat with friends to Horn Island, a barrier island off Mississippi's Gulf Coast, to celebrate Independence Day. Search crews spent two days looking for the teenager before a National Park Service ranger found his body near the island's northwestern tip on July 6. Authorities have not announced a cause or manner of death, and the investigation remains ongoing.

The official autopsy has not yet been released, making Wednesday's announcement the first independent medical findings expected to be shared publicly.

SEARCH CALLED OFF AFTER BODY MATCHING MISSING TEEN NOLAN WELLS FOUND OFF MISSISSIPPI COAST

The case has drawn growing national attention, with prominent civil rights leaders and public figures rallying behind Wells' family, including Crump, Sharpton, former NFL quarterback Colin Kaepernick and filmmaker Tyler Perry.

FOLLOW THE FOX TRUE CRIME TEAM ON X

Along with Crump, Sharpton has repeatedly called for a thorough investigation, though he has cautioned against drawing premature conclusions.

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST TRUE CRIME NEWS

"We cannot say it was racist, but we cannot say it was not racist," Sharpton said during Wells' funeral. "So let's not judge either way until all the evidence is in."

LIKE WHAT YOU'RE READING? CLICK HERE FOR MORE

Sharpton also questioned why Wells' cellphone and keys were found with his friends, why they did not immediately contact law enforcement after realizing he was missing and why local officials appeared quick to dismiss the possibility of foul play.

GOT A TIP?

During Wells' funeral Monday, Sharpton announced a $50,000 reward through the National Action Network for information leading to an arrest and conviction, saying someone at the July 4 gathering knows what happened.

"He was not on that island by himself," Sharpton said. "Somebody saw something and knows something. So I didn't come to make money. I came to give money. Stand up and tell the truth."

LISTEN TO THE NEW 'CRIME & JUSTICE WITH DONNA ROTUNNO' PODCAST

The Associated Press reported that Perry later pledged to help fund the reward in addition to paying for Wells' funeral. Crump has also said the local prosecutor agreed to present the completed investigation to a grand jury once it is finished and that the family was still awaiting the results of the independent autopsy following Monday's funeral.

GET BREAKING NEWS BY EMAIL

As the family continues pressing for answers, Fox News Digital has filed public records requests with the Jackson County Sheriff's Office and the Mississippi Department of Marine Resources' Marine Patrol seeking 911 calls, dispatch records, body-camera footage, incident reports, radio communications, vessel logs and other records related to the response to Wells' disappearance and death.

Fox News Digital has also reached out to Crump's office and the National Action Network for additional information regarding Wednesday's announcement and the reward.

item.title: RFK Jr says source of cyclospora outbreak identified, declares parasite outbreak 'under control'
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. says the cyclospora outbreak is under control and the source identified, but the FDA has yet to confirm a positive lab result.
item.content:encoded:
Health and Human Services (HHS) Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. said Tuesday that federal officials have brought the multistate cyclospora outbreak "under control," saying investigators have identified the source of the illnesses and implemented recalls, even as the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) continues investigating the precise source of the contamination.

"We do have the outbreak under control," Kennedy said during a healthcare fraud briefing Tuesday. "We have done extensive forensics, epidemiological forensics and we have identified the source of the outbreak. We, and the companies that are involved, have implemented a recall."

Kennedy's comments came two days after the FDA said an earlier laboratory result indicating a Taylor Farms lettuce sample tested positive for cyclospora should be considered a false positive following an additional laboratory review.

The FDA said it continues investigating the outbreak and collecting product samples. While traceback investigators identified Taylor Farms de Mexico as the supplier of shredded iceberg lettuce served at Taco Bell restaurants linked to the outbreak, the agency said no product samples connected to the investigation have produced a confirmed positive result for the parasite.

WHITE HOUSE ADDRESSES GROWING PARASITE OUTBREAK AFTER MORE THAN 400 AMERICANS SICKENED

Taylor Fresh Foods said the FDA informed the company the initial laboratory finding was incorrect and that it voluntarily recalled iceberg lettuce sourced from central Mexico on July 17, after investigators linked the product to Taco Bell locations where many sick people reported eating before becoming ill.

The company said the recall was limited to iceberg lettuce grown and processed in central Mexico and did not include Taylor Farms-branded salad products sold at retail.

The outbreak has sickened 1,644 people and hospitalized 94 across Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio and West Virginia. Federal investigators continue working to determine the contaminated food responsible for the illnesses.

Cyclospora infections have historically been linked to fresh produce, including leafy greens, herbs and berries, though officials have not confirmed a contaminated product through laboratory testing in this investigation.

DOCTORS WARN YOUR 'STOMACH BUG' MAY ACTUALLY BE A PARASITE THAT'S HARDER TO DETECT

Cyclospora is a parasite that can cause prolonged diarrhea, stomach cramps, fatigue and nausea. Some patients require hospitalization, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

Kennedy also rejected criticism of the administration's response after a reporter asked whether cuts to the Foodborne Diseases Active Surveillance Network, known as FoodNet, affected the government's ability to investigate the outbreak.

"We did cuts in the FoodNet program, but they were for redundant surveillance, and all the states will tell you that we're still doing forensics," Kennedy said.

He added that surveillance efforts continue nationwide and called criticism of the administration's response "invalid."

item.title: Eagles coach Nick Sirianni is turning to AI to help deliver new messaging: report
item.description: Philadelphia Eagles coach Nick Sirianni, who is entering his sixth season as head coach, reportedly has used ChatGPT to help with his messaging.
item.content:encoded:
Philadelphia Eagles head coach Nick Sirianni is the latest to get in on AI.

Sirianni, 45, is heading into his sixth season as coach of the Eagles and is looking for ways to keep his message fresh. So, the Super Bowl-winning coach turned to ChatGPT, even if he doesn’t know what all the letters stand for.

"We’ve created stories, things that people are looking up on ChatGP — fill me in on the letters," Sirianni said in June during a roundtable interview, according to The Athletic.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGEON FOXNEWS.COM

"What keeps it more fresh than what we’ve done?"

Sirianni and the Eagles’ mantra is "tough, detailed, together," but the head coach wants to find new ways to deliver messages that drum up inspiration.

So, his prompt?

ZERO BS. JUST DAKICH. TAKE THE DON'T @ ME PODCAST ON THE ROAD. DOWNLOAD NOW!

"Give me a great relentless effort true sports story," Sirianni wrote into the AI chatbot, according to The Athletic.

Sirianni then learned about an Arizona State wrestler born with one leg, Anthony Robles, who won a national championship in 2011. He planned to tell Robles’ story to the team as a way to reinforce the same message through a different story.

Whatever the message Sirianni decides on, it needs to be the right one. Despite winning the Super Bowl in 2024, the Eagles disappointed the following year, losing in the wild-card round while making many headlines throughout the season for their disconnect.

While a Super Bowl win may give most coaches more cushion in other cities, Philadelphia operates differently. Doug Pederson was fired three years after leading the Eagles to their first Super Bowl title in the 2017-18 season.

A slow start for Sirianni could lead to him being on the hot seat in the fall.

Follow Fox News Digital’s sports coverage on X, and subscribe to the Fox News Sports Huddle newsletter.

item.title: WATCH: Smithsonian history museum director stumbles over basic questions on sex: ‘I’m not sure’
item.description: Smithsonian director Anthea Hartig appeared stumped when Rep. Nancy Mace pressed her on gender questions tied to drag queen displays at the museum.
item.content:encoded:
Smithsonian National Museum of American History Director Anthea Hartig appeared stumped by a basic question on sex and gender from Rep. Nancy Mace, R-S.C.

During a House hearing on alleged "extreme political activism" on display at the Smithsonian, Mace asked Hartig, "Do you believe that men can become women?"

Hartig hesitated before answering, "Ma'am, as the director of the museum, that's not relevant in this case."

"It's very relevant," Mace shot back. Motioning to images of Smithsonian displays featuring drag queens, Mace continued, "You've got men dressed up as women, and you have them on displays in front of kids. Do you think men can become women? Yes or no?"

DISCRIMINATORY 'SHADOW' REVIEW PROCESS LOCKS WHITE, ASIAN STUDENTS OUT OF CALIFORNIA MED SCHOOL, DOJ ALLEGES

Hartig did not answer the question but instead began to discuss one of the drag queen displays pictured on the board behind Mace. The congresswoman interrupted her.

"What is a woman, Dr. Hartig?" Mace demanded.

Hartig began to answer that "as a director and as a historian, it's not my-" before Mace cut her off again, saying, "You sexualized the American History Museum. So, this is a fair question. What is a woman?"

Looking down for a moment, Hartig responded, "We strive to represent and not categorize all Americans."

"OK, I have another question for you," Mace continued. "There's a glossary at the museum that informs visitors that while American culture traditionally recognizes two genders, other countries recognize three or more."

"So, enlighten me this morning: how many genders are there?" Mace asked.

Hartig hesitated again before asking, "Ma’am, which glossary?"

"This is a display, and it has a glossary that says other countries recognize three or more genders. So, I would like you to enlighten me today; you're the director of the American History Museum, who's sexualized things in front of kids. I want to know, I want to ask you, how many genders are there?"

Finally, Hartig responded, "I'm not sure which glossary you're speaking," before adding, "I’m happy to get back to you about that glossary."

ANTI-ICE WALKOUT EXPOSES HOW SCHOOL DISTRICT 'FAILED' PARENTS, RISKED STUDENT SAFETY: 'BROKEN MY TRUST'

Mace pressed further, saying, "One of your exhibits at the American History Museum says sometimes gender isn't revealed at birth. So, when is gender revealed?"

"When was your gender revealed to you, Dr. Hartig?" she added.

Smiling, Hartig simply answered, "I don't really think that's relevant to the conversation."

"It is relevant. You have a display at the American History Museum talking about, ‘Gender isn't always revealed at birth.’ So, when was your gender revealed?" insisted Mace.

Hartig began to answer by saying, "So, since I am a Ph.D.-" before Mace yielded the floor back to the committee chair.

Earlier this month, a sweeping new White House report concluded that the Smithsonian Institution's National Museum of American History has become a taxpayer-backed institution of "ideological capture" and "extreme political activism."

According to the 162-page "Restoring Truth and Sanity to American History" report, delivered on July 4, 2026, the museum treats the American story as a political tool rather than a shared national inheritance.

"The report concludes that the Smithsonian Institution, and the National Museum of American History in particular, under its current leadership and current interpretive ideology, cannot be trusted to tell America's story honestly and in a way that is inspiring, unifying, and worthy of our great republic," the White House Domestic Policy Council wrote in its July 4 report following up on President Donald Trump’s March 27 executive order. "By the intention and at the direction of current Museum and Smithsonian leadership, NMAH has become subject to institutional capture by a radical, activist ideology that is fundamentally opposed to telling the noble, honest story of the great country we know and love."

FAR-LEFT ACTIVIST CONFERENCE AT TEACHERS UNION HQ RAISES CLASSROOM CONCERNS

"At the Smithsonian’s National Museum of American History, visitors young and old should encounter the story of the United States told with honesty, seriousness, and pride," the report's executive summary began. "In particular, it should help the American people understand where America came from, what makes it distinctive, and why it is worth preserving."

Hartig said in her opening statement that "the Smithsonian takes the concerns raised in the White House Domestic Policy Council's report seriously, and as we continue to fact-check the report, we unwaveringly attest that it does not fairly or accurately characterize the full body of work at the museum."

SIGN UP TO GET THE POLITICS NEWSLETTER

She asserted that "the museum does not take sides in America's political debates," but instead, "We preserve and document the evidence of American life in all of its breadth, so that the public can encounter the past and draw their own conclusions."

"There is always room for improvement, but I also know the beauty and the inspiration and the expertise that lies in our collections, our exhibits, and our programming," said Hartig.

Fox News Digital reached out to the Smithsonian Institution for additional comment.

Fox News Digital’s Eric Mack contributed to this report.

item.title: Agent Rich Paul says LeBron James won't be rushed on NBA free agency decision despite outside pressure
item.description: Rich Paul said LeBron James has all the information he needs from teams, but won't be rushed into a free-agent decision on anyone else's timeline.
item.content:encoded:
LeBron James’ agent Rich Paul won’t be putting any specific timeline on his client’s free-agent decision.

They’re in no rush despite how badly the basketball world wishes the NBA’s all-time scoring leader would make a choice.

"There’s been several people to call and say, ‘Hey, Rich, is there anything more we should be doing? We could be doing?' And my answer is, ‘No. We don’t need you to go above and beyond about anything. We understand it. It’s clear. All the messages have been sent,’" Paul said on the "Game Over" podcast with Max Kellerman.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

Paul is basically saying James has all the information he needs from prospective teams, enough so that he will be content with whatever choice he ends up making.

However, Paul stressed that James will do so when he’s ready. And Paul has no clue when, or where, that’s going to happen.

LEBRON JAMES TELLS LOS ANGELES LAKERS HE WILL LEAVE IN FREE AGENCY AFTER EIGHT SEASONS: REPORT

"These people don’t know anything. I don’t know when his choice is going to be made, but it could be 48 seconds [from now]," Paul explained. "It could be 48 minutes. I don’t know. Again, you can’t make somebody know something that they don’t know."

It’s gotten to the point where James’ free-agent decision has caused drama throughout the NBA world, as anticipation rises with every day that passes without word.

At Fanatics Fest this past weekend, James made several appearances, where crowds flooded the Javits Center in Manhattan to see if he would reveal where he’s going. They were disappointed when he had nothing to reveal.

Even NBA Commissioner Adam Silver mentioned that James’ decision is holding up the league’s schedule for next season. Paul mentioned that he met with Silver to discuss that situation, reiterating that James will make the decision when he’s ready – not because of any other factor.

The arguable basketball "G.O.A.T." helped the Lakers earn the No. 4 seed in a strong Western Conference this past season, averaging 20.9 points, 7.2 assists and 6.1 rebounds across 60 games. And though Luka Doncic was unavailable in the playoffs, James was able to lead the Lakers past the Houston Rockets in the first round before a sweep to the Oklahoma City Thunder in May.

James will turn 42 during the 2026-27 NBA season, but the 22-time All-Star and four-time MVP continues to prove his worth.

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST SPORTS NEWS

But, for those believing he’s dragging out yet another decision longer than it needs to be, Paul is stern with his response: James won’t be rushed.

The league – the world – will just have to keep waiting for the right time.

Follow Fox News Digital’s sports coverage on X and subscribe to the Fox News Sports Huddle newsletter.
